related_questions.question: Why is therapy so expensive in Canada?
related_questions.snippet: There are two main reasons therapy can feel pricey: individual private therapy (i.e. services that aren't medically necessary) are not covered by Canadian health coverage plans so individuals are most often required to pay out-of-pocket or be covered by individual insurance, and licensed counsellors are highly trained ...
related_questions.title: Why is Therapy So Expensive in Canada? (Professional Therapy)

related_questions.question: What is the difference between counselling and therapy?

text_blocks.snippet: Counseling typically offers short-term, solution-focused help for specific current problems (like stress or a bad breakup), providing coping tools, while therapy (psychotherapy) is often longer-term and delves deeper into complex issues, past experiences, and patterns, aiming for lasting change in areas like trauma, depression, or identity, though the terms are often used interchangeably and a professional may offer both. The key difference often lies in depth: counseling guides you through immediate challenges, whereas therapy explores root causes for profound growth.
snippet_highlighted_words: Counseling typically offers short-term, solution-focused help for specific current problems (like stress or a bad breakup), providing coping tools, while therapy (psychotherapy) is often longer-term and delves deeper into complex issues, past experiences, and patterns, aiming for lasting change in areas like trauma, depression, or identity

list.snippet: Focus: Specific, current problems (e.g., career stress, grief, relationship issues).
Goal: Develop strategies, coping mechanisms, and immediate solutions.
Approach: Structured, action-oriented, and focused on problem-solving.
Focus: Deeper, more complex, or chronic mental health issues (e.g., trauma, anxiety, depression, personality disorders).
Goal: Understand patterns, explore past experiences, and facilitate significant personal growth.
Approach: In-depth, exploratory, examining thoughts, behaviors, and past roots.

list.title: Overlap:
list.snippet: A therapist can provide counseling, but a counselor might not have the training for complex psychotherapy.

list.title: Choice:
list.snippet: Counseling is great for managing a crisis; therapy is for deep, lasting change.

list.title: Best Fit:
list.snippet: Your needs (specific issue vs. deep-seated patterns) and the professional's qualifications matter most.

related_questions.question: How much do you pay for counseling?
related_questions.snippet: R600 – R1900/session On average, expect to be charged between R600/session and R1900/session for individual therapy.
related_questions.title: How much does therapy cost? - Procompare.co.za

related_questions.question: What if we can't afford therapy?
related_questions.snippet: Ask your therapist about their sliding scale options Many therapists will adjust their hourly fee to match your income or base it on the financial resources you have to put towards therapy. You just have to ask. It can also be an option if you've already been seeing a therapist and need to reevaluate your spending.
related_questions.title: I can't afford therapy! - MHA Screening - Mental Health America

related_questions.question: What is the 2 year rule in therapy?
related_questions.snippet: The 2-year rule is APA's way of acknowledging that life holds few absolutes; many continua need to be considered. Thus, the Ethics Code includes an absolute prohibition against sex with former clients for a period of two years following termination.
related_questions.title: Sex With Former Therapy Clients: The 2-Year Rule - Psi Chi

related_questions.question: Is there a way to get free therapy in Canada?
related_questions.snippet: There are free, accessible mental health resources available across Canada to help—whether you're facing stress, anxiety, grief, or simply need someone to talk to. If you're in crisis and need immediate support, call or text 988 or visit 988lifeline.org to chat with a trained counselor.
related_questions.title: 12 Free Mental Health Resources in Canada You Should Know About

related_questions.title: What is counselling vs therapy? - University of Greater Manchester

related_questions.question: Is counselling covered by insurance in Canada?
related_questions.snippet: If your extended health care (EHC) benefit includes coverage for mental health practitioners, you also have access to mental health & counselling services.
related_questions.title: Mental health & counselling services - Group Benefits Manulife

related_questions.question: Is 20 sessions of therapy a lot?
related_questions.snippet: So how long does it typically take for treatment to work? Recent research indicates that on average 15 to 20 sessions are required for 50 percent of patients to recover as indicated by self-reported symptom measures.
related_questions.title: How Long Will It Take for Treatment to Work?

related_questions.question: Do Canadians get free therapy?
related_questions.snippet: Without insurance, individual therapy with a psychologist, psychiatrist, or social worker could cost anywhere from $125–225, which is unaffordable for many Canadians. That said, many community organizations, federally funded programs for First Nations and Inuit people, and crisis support hotlines provide no-cost care.
related_questions.title: Free Therapy In Canada For Mental Health | BetterHelp
